# tmuxxer config

tmuxxer reads its main config from:

```text
$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/tmuxxer/config
```

If `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is not set, it uses:

```text
~/.config/tmuxxer/config
```

Run `tmuxxer init` to create or rewrite project roots interactively. Run `tmuxxer config path` to print the exact path.

## TOML v2 format

New configs are written as TOML v2:

```toml
# Generated by tmuxxer setup

version = 2

[sources]
sessions = true
directories = true
docker = true

[session]
name_strategy = "path"

[docker]
new_session = true

[search]
ignore = ["target", ".git"]

[[search.roots]]
path = "~/code"
depth = 1

[[search.roots]]
path = "~/work"
depth = 3
```

Unknown TOML keys are rejected. `version` is optional and defaults to `2`, but any explicit version other than `2` is rejected.

## Flags

| Key | Type | Default | Values | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `version` | integer | `2` | `2` | Config format version. Optional. |
| `sources.sessions` | boolean | `true` | `true`, `false` | Show existing tmux sessions in the picker. |
| `sources.directories` | boolean | `true` | `true`, `false` | Show scanned project directories in the picker. |
| `sources.docker` | boolean | `true` | `true`, `false` | Show running Docker containers in the picker when Docker is available. |
| `session.name_strategy` | string | `"path"` | `"path"`, `"basename"` | Controls generated tmux session names for directory entries. |
| `docker.new_session` | boolean | `true` | `true`, `false` | Open selected Docker containers in their own tmux sessions. When `false`, tmuxxer opens Docker shells directly in the current pane. |
| `search.ignore` | string array | `[]` | patterns | Paths or path components to skip while scanning directory roots. |
| `search.roots[].path` | string | none | path | Directory root to scan. `~` and `~/...` are expanded from `$HOME`. Required for each root. |
| `search.roots[].depth` | integer | `1` | `1` or greater | Scan depth for a root. `0` is treated as `1`. |

At least one source must be enabled. If `sources.directories = true`, at least one `[[search.roots]]` entry is required.

## Boolean Values

In TOML config, booleans must be `true` or `false`.

When using `tmuxxer config set`, boolean values also accept:

```text
true, yes, y, 1, on
false, no, n, 0, off
```

## Session Name Strategy

`session.name_strategy = "path"` is the default. It uses the directory basename plus a stable path hash, so projects with the same folder name get different tmux sessions.

`session.name_strategy = "basename"` uses the legacy basename-only behavior. Dots are replaced with underscores, so different projects with the same basename can collide.

## Ignore Patterns

`search.ignore` supports simple `*` wildcards.

Examples:

```toml
[search]
ignore = [
  "target",
  ".git",
  ".*",
  "node_modules/*",
  "~/work/tmp",
]
```

Matching rules:

- No slash: matches any path component, for example `target` or `.*`.
- Slash without leading `/`: matches relative paths anywhere below each search root, for example `node_modules/*`.
- Leading `~/` or `/`: matches from that absolute path prefix.
- `./folder/` is normalized to `folder`.

Use `tmuxxer --ignore PATTERN...` to toggle ignore patterns from the command line, or `tmuxxer --ignore` for the interactive prompt.

## Search Roots

Each `[[search.roots]]` entry needs a `path`. `depth` is optional and defaults to `1`.

```toml
[[search.roots]]
path = "~/code"
depth = 1
```

Depth controls how far below the root tmuxxer lists directories. `1` means immediate child directories only.

Use `tmuxxer --add PATH...` to toggle search roots without re-running the setup wizard. Roots added this way use `depth = 1`.

## Config CLI

These keys can be read and changed with `tmuxxer config get`, `set`, and `toggle`:

```text
sources.sessions
sources.directories
sources.docker
docker.new_session
session.name_strategy
```

Only boolean keys can be toggled.

`tmuxxer config list` also prints `search.ignore` and all `search.roots` values for inspection. Search roots and ignores are changed with `tmuxxer --add`, `tmuxxer --ignore`, `tmuxxer init`, or by editing the config file.

Useful commands:

```bash
tmuxxer config path
tmuxxer config list
tmuxxer config get sources.docker
tmuxxer config set sources.docker false
tmuxxer config set session.name_strategy basename
tmuxxer config toggle sources.docker
tmuxxer config validate
tmuxxer config migrate
```

`tmuxxer config validate` checks syntax, supported keys, supported values, and required settings without opening the picker.

`tmuxxer config migrate` rewrites the loaded config as TOML v2 without changing behavior.

## Legacy config

Legacy key/value configs are still loaded:

```ini
docker_new_session = true
path = ~/code
depth = 1
ignore = target
```

Legacy keys:

| Key | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `path` | Adds a search root. |
| `depth` | Sets the previous path depth, or the default depth for following paths when used before any path. |
| `ignore` | Adds one ignore pattern. |
| `docker_new_session` | Maps to `docker.new_session`. |

Legacy configs default all picker sources to enabled and `session.name_strategy` to `"path"`. Any command that saves config rewrites the file as TOML v2.
